title: Ehretia retusa
taxonomy: Ehretia retusa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Ehretia, which comprises around 50 species of plants. The genus Ehretia is part of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Ehretia retusa is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and Australia. The plant is also known as the 'Native Fiddlewood' and is valued for its timber and ornamental value.

intro: Ehretia retusa, also known as the Koa haole or Island ehretia, is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae, native to the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities, which can help in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ehretia retusa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ehretia retusa is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin disorders.
